name: Sourdough Starter
recipeIngredient: 1 package active dry yeast or 1 cake fresh yeast
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the yeast in 2 cups of warm water.
2. Add the 2 cups of flour to create a paste-like consistency.
3. Transfer the mixture to a bowl made of glass, crockery, or pottery (not metal).
4. Place the bowl in a warm place and let it sit for 3-4 days.
5. When the mixture begins to ferment, skim off any foam on top.
6. Adjust the consistency by adding more flour and water as needed.
7. After making the starter, you can use it for up to 3 days at room temperature or about a week in the refrigerator (never freeze).
8. To keep the yeast active, add flour and water gradually (no more than 1 cup of sifted flour and 1 cup of warm water per cup of sour dough). Mix until smooth.
9. Let the mixture rise for 3 hours.
10. In about 2 days, increase the sour dough using the same method if desired, continuing to skim off the foam as it works.
